summary: Matched and renamed a large set of VS Records mndiagram functions, expanded mndiagram.c substantially, and propagated clearer persistent stats/name-tag types through gm headers and call sites. The PR also renamed multiple symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt from address/fn-style names to behavior names such as mnDiagram_GetHitPercentage, mnDiagram_FormatTime, mnDiagram_InputProc, and mnDiagram_UpdateScrollArrowVisibility. Review focused on enforcing module prefixes for non-static functions, moving user-data layouts into mn/types.h, keeping link-sensitive definitions near the top or in .static.h, and recognizing an inline of mnDiagram_UpdateScrollArrowVisibility.
what_changed: Expanded src/melee/mn/mndiagram.c from a mostly stubbed file into a much larger decompilation slice, adding over two thousand lines and matching/naming many mndiagram helper and proc functions.

Renamed mndiagram symbol entries in config/GALE01/symbols.txt from address-based names such as mnDiagram_8023EA2C and fn_8023FE30 to descriptive names such as mnDiagram_GetFighterByIndex, mnDiagram_GetNameByIndex, mnDiagram_InputProc, mnDiagram_PopupCleanup, mnDiagram_OnFrame, and mnDiagram_CursorProc.
Renamed generic/global helpers in the mn range, including mn_8022E950 to mn_IsFighterUnlocked, mn_8022EB24 to mn_GetDigitAt, and mn_8022EB78 to mn_GetDigitCount.

Introduced or used clearer persistent game data type names in gm code, replacing anonymous/offset-style names like gmm_x1F2C and gmm_x2FF8_inner with FighterData, NameTagDataBank, and NameTagData in gmmain_lib.c, gmmain_lib.h, gm_17BA.c, and gm/types.h.
Updated GetPersistentFighterData to return struct FighterData* instead of void*, and GetPersistentNameData to return struct NameTagData* instead of an offset-style struct.
Adjusted GameRules initializer formatting in gmmain_lib.c from nested anonymous initializer blocks to explicit scalar fields with comments such as unk_xc, xD, xE, xF, unk_x10, x11, x12, and x13.
Added mndiagram-related declarations and forward/type plumbing across mndiagram.h, mndiagram.static.h, mn/types.h, mndiagram2.h, mndiagram3.h, mnmain.h, mnname.h, and forward.h.
Added baselib/gobjproc.h support needed by the mndiagram work.
smart_moves: Used Dolphin debugging to infer behavior-based names for many menu/stat functions instead of leaving address-only names.
Converted high-traffic persistent stats accessors to typed returns, making downstream decomp in mndiagram and gm code less dependent on casts and opaque offset structs.

Captured display-specific behavior in names such as mnDiagram_IsDistanceOverflow and mnDiagram_ConvertDistanceForDisplay, including locale-dependent US vs non-US distance thresholds evident in the diff excerpt.
Grouped related VS Records/stat helpers under the mnDiagram_ module prefix after review feedback, improving symbol searchability and consistency.

Added static asserts for inferred mndiagram structs in the excerpt, which is useful for preserving layout assumptions while decompiling code that overlays arrays, archive pointers, and GObj user data.
Identified likely proc/callback roles in formerly fn_ symbols, e.g. PopupInputProc, InputProc, PopupCleanup, PopupAnimProc, ExitAnimProc, OnFrame, and CursorProc.
decomp_lessons: For this codebase, non-static functions should be prefixed with the module name; the reviewer explicitly said to use mnDiagram_ for non-static mndiagram functions.
When a GObj user-data layout becomes known, prefer adding a named type in the appropriate module types header, here mn/types.h, rather than keeping ad hoc local structs in the C file.
Typed persistent-save structures are worth propagating through accessors and callers once the layout is understood; this reduces casts and clarifies all later stat reads/writes.
Keep link-sensitive definitions near the top of the file or in the module .static.h when linking/matching is easier that way.
Look for repeated helper logic that may actually be an inline; one reviewer noted code looked like an inline of mnDiagram_UpdateScrollArrowVisibility.
Behavioral names from in-game debugging are valuable but should still respect repository naming conventions before merge.
Large AI-assisted decomp output still needed manual cleanup and match regression fixes; the author noted broken/regressed matches would be fixed.
naming_conventions: Use mnDiagram_ as the prefix for all non-static functions in the mndiagram module.

Use descriptive PascalCase after the module prefix for mndiagram functions, as shown by mnDiagram_GetFighterByIndex, mnDiagram_GetPlayPercentage, mnDiagram_FormatDecimalNumber, and mnDiagram_UpdateScrollArrowVisibility.
Use mn_ for broader menu helpers outside the specific mndiagram module, as shown by mn_IsFighterUnlocked, mn_GetDigitAt, and mn_GetDigitCount.
Prefer domain types such as FighterData, NameTagData, and NameTagDataBank over offset-style temporary names such as gmm_x1F2C, gmm_x2FF8, and gmm_x2FF8_inner.
Proc/callback functions were named with role suffixes such as InputProc, PopupInputProc, PopupCleanup, PopupAnimProc, ExitAnimProc, OnFrame, and CursorProc.
The review suggested a simple user-data type name such as Diagram in mn/types.h, though the final exact type name is not fully evidenced by the excerpt.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used Dolphin debugging to assign behavior names to matched functions.
Used STATIC_ASSERT on inferred structs to lock sizes such as 0x1C, 0x78, 0x108, and 0x50 in the diff excerpt.
Represented overlapping or callback-specific user data with overlay structs in the excerpt, such as cleanup data accessing only text objects and anim data accessing only a JObj at a known offset.
Preserved array dimensions and modulo/division patterns for name data banking, including indexing name data by arg0 / 19 and arg0 % 19.

Matched display conversion logic with exact constants and casts, including US distance overflow threshold 0x274A6, non-US threshold 0x186A0, division by 160934 or 100000, and a floating conversion by about 30.4788.
Flattened a struct initializer to named scalar fields where that better matched the actual GameRules layout.
Reviewer feedback indicates that moving definitions to the top of the file or .static.h can make linking easier.

review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime
review_feedback.comment: Asked whether all newly named functions should be prefixed with mn_ because existing names were inconsistent.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: All non-static functions should be prefixed with the module name, mnDiagram_.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: The user data should be a new type in mn/types.h, maybe called Diagram for now.

review_feedback.author: unknown from excerpt
review_feedback.comment: When linking, definitions are easier if placed at the top of the file or in .static.h.

review_feedback.author: unknown from excerpt
review_feedback.comment: A code pattern looked like an inline of mnDiagram_UpdateScrollArrowVisibility.

review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime
review_feedback.comment: Acknowledged broken and regressed matches and said they would be fixed.

follow_up_queries: Check final src/melee/mn/types.h for the exact mndiagram user-data type name chosen after review.
Search for remaining unprefixed non-static mndiagram functions and rename them to mnDiagram_* if evidence supports it.
Look for repeated call sites or duplicated code around mnDiagram_UpdateScrollArrowVisibility that should use an inline.
Audit mndiagram2 and mndiagram3 for the same module-prefix and user-data/header lessons, since the PR body says work would continue there.
Verify whether any ugly 70-90% unmatched functions mentioned in the PR body were later cleaned up or split into smaller follow-up PRs.
Search for remaining gmm_x1F2C, gmm_x2FF8, and gmm_x2FF8_inner uses to propagate FighterData, NameTagDataBank, and NameTagData consistently.
